data.title: Bitcoin rises to $91,500, Brazilian stocks hit record highs. Cryptocurrency market fluctuates, Bitcoin down 17% this month.
data.description: Global markets experienced a quiet session on Thursday with Wall Street closed for Thanksgiving, putting the focus on cryptocurrencies. Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) saw a 1.1% increase to $91,500 in New York, aiming for its fourth gain in five sessions after a recent dip to $80,000. Ethereum (CRYPTO: ETH) dropped 0.3% to $3,000 following five days of gains, and Solana (CRYPTO: SOL) slipped 0.7% to $141.9. Bitcoin has declined by nearly 17% this month, marking its worst performance since February, while Ethereum has lost over 20%. In Europe, equities saw modest movements.

data.title: Producer inflation rises to 0.3% in September, fueled by energy costs, while retail sales weaken, indicating potential Fed rate cut.
data.description: In September, producer inflation rebounded to a 0.3% increase month-over-month as energy prices surged, leading to a 2.7% rise year-over-year. The Producer Price Index (PPI) met expectations, reversing the previous month's 0.1% decline. Gasoline prices saw an 11.8% spike, driving overall production costs, while food prices also rose by 1.1%. Excluding food and energy, goods saw a modest 0.2% increase. Core PPI, excluding food, energy, and trade services, ticked up by 0.1%. Retail sales growth fell short of projections, indicating consumer spending fatigue, potentially prompting a Fed interest-rate cut. Final demand goods saw the largest increase since February 2024, surging by 0.9%. The data, released by the Bureau of Labor Statistics, supports expectations for a possible rate cut by the Federal Reserve in the upcoming month.

data.title: Bitfury invests $50 million in Gonka, a decentralized network for AI compute, empowering users with hardware to contribute to AI workloads.
data.description: A technology incubator called Bitfury recently revealed its plan to invest $50 million in Gonka, a decentralized network focusing on high-efficiency AI computation. This network allows individuals with various hardware capacities, from a single GPU to a complete data center, to directly contribute to significant AI tasks like model training and inference. This investment signifies a departure from traditional centralized AI computation providers, who restrict access through APIs and corporate channels.

data.title: Unions push for Pentagon employee rights, seek bargaining rights in military's legislation amendments.
data.description: Federal sector unions are backing a push to attain bargaining rights for Pentagon staff as legislators make final adjustments to the military's crucial legislation.

data.title: Morien reveals non-brokered financing for LIFE,raising $2 million to support growth and development.
data.description: In recent news, it has been reported that the national unemployment rate has dropped to 4.3%, the lowest it has been in five years. This decrease in unemployment is seen as a positive indicator of economic growth and job market stability. It is also noted that the stock market has been performing well, with the main indexes reaching record highs. Additionally, the latest data shows that the GDP has grown by 6.2% in the last quarter, surpassing expectations and demonstrating a strong recovery from the impacts of the pandemic. These numbers suggest a promising outlook for the country's economy and employment opportunities.

data.title: Government shutdown ended, NLRB faces renewed focus amid controversies protecting private sector workers.
data.description: The recent conclusion of the longest government shutdown in history has shifted attention towards the controversies linked to the federal agency responsible for safeguarding the rights of private sector employees. This development opens the door for a reinvigorated examination of the issues at hand.

data.title: Dow Jones and major indexes rise; Palantir and Nvidia climb as one biotech company falls.
data.description: Today, the Dow Jones Industrial Average along with other key indexes closed the session with gains. The Dow rebounded, and notable companies like Palantir and Nvidia saw an uptick in their stock prices, while a biotech company experienced a decline. This live coverage discussed the market movement for the day without specifying the source.

data.title: Vinci, a pioneer in Physics-Driven AI for hardware design, emerges from stealth with $46M in funding.
data.description: A tech company based in Palo Alto, California, Vinci, known for its Physics-Driven AI technology for hardware design and simulation, recently revealed itself to the public and disclosed a total funding of $46 million. The funding consists of a Series A round primarily backed by Xora Innovation and a Seed round led by Eclipse. Vinci showcased their innovative AI system that mimics a group of hardware engineers, capable of executing numerous validated simulations within hours rather than weeks. Founded by computational geometry specialist Hardik Kabaria, the company is focused on revolutionizing hardware design processes.

data.title: Federal Reserve ends Quantitative Tightening, freeze balance sheet at $6.57 trillion, impacting BTC. BTC at $86,600, down 30% from peak. $1 billion in leveraged crypto positions liquidated. Fed's move compared to 2019.
data.description: The Federal Reserve concluded its Quantitative Tightening program on December 1, 2025, keeping its balance sheet at $6.57 trillion. This marked a significant shift in monetary policy, with $2.39 trillion drained from the financial system since June 2022. The Fed stopped Treasury security runoff but continues reducing mortgage-backed securities at $35 billion monthly. Bank reserves are at $2.89 trillion, nearing critical levels. This liquidity withdrawal was the largest in central banking history, impacting risk assets. Bitcoin is around $86,600, down 30% from its peak. Nearly $1 billion in leveraged crypto positions were liquidated during a recent selloff, showing how thin liquidity heightens volatility. Analysts compare this event to a similar one in August 2019. The full story is available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Stocks rise, Dow up for Nov, Nvidia falls; Intel, Tesla and Robinhood in spotlight. Inflation data delayed. (100)
data.description: Friday saw a rise in the stock market as key players like Intel, Nvidia, Tesla, and Robinhood were in the spotlight. The Dow Jones Industrial Average posted positive gains for the month of November, although Nvidia experienced a decline. Investors are also anticipating delayed inflation data.

data.title: Stellantis faces default for relocating Jeep production to the U.S. per Canada. The move jeopardizes contracts worth millions.
data.description: In efforts to appease President Trump, the car manufacturer relocated production from Ontario. However, the company failed to honor contracts involving hundreds of millions of dollars in aid, according to Canadian officials.
